ddls:
CREATE TABLE "repayment_schedule" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each repayment schedule */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the loan application */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date of the repayment */,
  "payment_amount" REAL /* Amount of the repayment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collections" (
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collection */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the loan application */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date of the collection */,
  "collection_amount" REAL /* Amount of the collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "late_fees" (
  "late_fee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each late fee */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the loan application */,
  "late_fee_date" TEXT /* Date when the late fee was applied */,
  "late_fee_amount" REAL /* Amount of the late fee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("late_fee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "credit_score" (
  "credit_score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each credit score */,
  "borrower_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the borrower */,
  "credit_score_date" TEXT /* Date when the credit score was pulled */,
  "credit_score" INTEGER /* Credit score of the borrower */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("credit_score_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each repayment schedule
Unique identifier for each late fee
Date when the late fee was applied
Unique identifier for each credit score
Date when the credit score was pulled
